name: Bembézar Trail
name_original: Santuario de los Ángeles y Embalse de Bembézar – Sierra de Hornachuelos
description: This moderate 7.2-mile jogging trail in Sierra de Hornachuelos follows the Bembézar Reservoir, offering scenic views and historical sights.
region_intro: Jogging routes around National Park Sierra de Hornachuelos traverse a diverse landscape characterized by massifs, ravines, and elevated plains. The region features well-preserved Mediterranean forests, primarily composed of holm oaks and cork oaks, alongside lush riverine ecosystems with willows and ash trees. Rivers like the Bembézar carve deep gorges, providing varied terrain for running. This protected area offers a mix of challenging paths and gentler routes through its natural environment. Best jogging routes around National Park Sierra de Hornachuelos The most popular jogging route is Bembézar Trail, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) moderate trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This route follows the Bembézar River, offering shaded paths through riverine forests. Another top favourite among local runners is Hornachuelos Old Town loop from Hornachuelos, a difficult 12.7 miles (20.5 km) path. This route explores the varied terrain around the historic town of Hornachuelos, including significant elevation changes. Local runners also love the Hornachuelos Old Town – Hornachuelos Old Town loop from Hornachuelos, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through the area surrounding Hornachuelos, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Jogging around National Park Sierra de Hornachuelos is defined by diverse massifs, riverine ecosystems, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in National Park Sierra de Hornachuelos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 250 runners have used komoot to explore National Park Sierra de Hornachuelos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in National Park Sierra de Hornachuelos?
A: National Park Sierra de Hornachuelos offers a comprehensive network of over 30 running routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, ranging from moderate paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation changes, ensuring a diverse experience for all runners.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in the park?
A: While the komoot guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, the region research indicates that the park does offer easier options. For a relaxed jog, consider trails like the 'Hiking loop from Almodóvar del Río' (5.3 km) or the 'Mirador de Pairejas loop from Almodóvar del Río' (5.1 km). The 'Sendero del Arroyo de la Rabilarga' (700m) and 'Sendero Botánico' (1.2 km) are also short, easy circular walks.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while running in Sierra de Hornachuelos?
A: Jogging routes in Sierra de Hornachuelos traverse a diverse landscape featuring massifs, ravines, and elevated plains. You'll experience well-preserved Mediterranean forests with holm oaks and cork oaks, alongside lush riverine ecosystems with willows and ash trees. Rivers like the Bembézar carve deep gorges, providing varied and scenic terrain. Many trails also offer breathtaking panoramas, such as views of the Bembézar River.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in National Park Sierra de Hornachuelos?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornachuelos Old Town – Hornachuelos Old Town loop from Hornachuelos&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.4 km long. For a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornachuelos Old Town loop from Hornachuelos&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 20.5 km path exploring the varied terrain around the historic town.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can discover along the running trails?
A: While running, you can discover several interesting landmarks and viewpoints. The area around the abandoned Monasterio de Santa María de los Ángeles offers stunning views of the Bembézar River. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5340608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Seminario de los Ángeles&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3334642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Bembézar River from the Santuario de los Ángeles&lt;/a&gt;. The park also features significant dams like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5617701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bembézar Dam&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/919273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornachuelos Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while jogging in the park?
A: Yes, National Park Sierra de Hornachuelos is known for its rich biodiversity. While jogging, visitors might encounter diverse wildlife, including red deer, wild boar, and various bird species. The park is particularly renowned for its significant colonies of griffon vultures and black vultures, making for an engaging experience.

Q: Is there parking available near the running routes?
A: The town of Hornachuelos serves as a main starting point for exploring the park. The Huerta del Rey Visitor Centre, located in Hornachuelos, offers information and is a good place to start, often providing parking facilities for visitors accessing the trails.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in National Park Sierra de Hornachuelos?
A: The running routes in National Park Sierra de Hornachuelos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Over 250 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, well-maintained paths, and the opportunity to experience the natural beauty of the Mediterranean forests and riverine ecosystems.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a more strenuous run, the park offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornachuelos Old Town loop from Cortijo del Pingarillo&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 22.6 km route with significant elevation changes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38171300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Porrada – Finca Loca loop from Villalobillos&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 23.6 km with over 500 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Sierra de Hornachuelos?
A: The park's lush riverine forests create shaded and refreshing paths, which are especially appealing during warmer months. However, the diverse topography and Mediterranean climate generally make it suitable for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, while winter can provide cooler, crisp conditions. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Is public transport an option to reach the running trails?
A: While specific public transport routes directly to trailheads are not detailed, the town of Hornachuelos is the main access point for the park. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or regional transport options to Hornachuelos, and then plan your route from there, potentially using the Visitor Centre at Huerta del Rey as a starting point.
tips: Population at the foot of the Sierra of the same name, in the Sierra Morena Cordoba. Its name comes from the Arabic "furnuyulu" (city of holes) and its layout is also of clear Arab influence with narrow and steep streets.

Nice reservoir on the Bembézar river. This is actually a bypass of the main reservoir further north. It is elongated in shape and with steep forest slopes on both sides. It is a common place among hikers, who can go all the way along a path that reaches the main reservoir.

The view of the Seminario de los Ángeles in Hornachuelos is one of the most impressive in the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park, especially when viewed from the path that runs along the Bembézar River.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Santuario de los Angeles y Embalse de Bembazar – Sierra de Hornachuelos trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in the Natural Park of Sierra de Hornachuelos. While specific parking details aren't provided, access points are typically found near the start of the route, often close to the Hornachuelos Old Town or the Seminario de Santa María de los Ángeles. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for designated parking areas.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally considered suitable for a relaxed outing, with a difficulty level classified as "medium-low" or "easy" by local sources. It features continuous ascents and descents but is largely without significant elevation changes, making it accessible for beginners and families looking for a scenic jog or walk.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Santuario de los Angeles y Embalse de Bembazar trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is primarily a path (sendero) that runs parallel to the Bembézar River and dam. You can expect a mix of dirt and natural surfaces, rather than paved sections. While generally smooth, there are slight inclines, particularly before reaching the "Montaña de los Ángeles." It is well-marked and maintained.

faqs.question: What notable sights or landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several points of interest. You'll jog alongside the beautiful blue waters of the Bembézar Reservoir and pass through Mediterranean forest. Key landmarks include the Seminario de Santa María de los Ángeles, an ancient Franciscan monastery, and the "Fuente de los Tres Caños" (Fountain of the Three Spouts), where tradition says drinking brings health, luck, and love. There are also great views, such as the View of the Seminario de los Ángeles.

faqs.question: Is the Santuario de los Angeles y Embalse de Bembazar trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Parque Natural Sierra de Hornachuelos, dogs are generally permitted. However, it is crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are often ideal due to milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. During these seasons, the Mediterranean forest is particularly beautiful, and the weather is more comfortable for jogging. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if visiting then.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Parque Natural Sierra de Hornachuelos, including this route. However, it's always a good idea to check the official park website or local information centers for any updated regulations, especially if planning activities beyond standard jogging or hiking.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while jogging?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park is rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, including Griffon vultures, the endangered Black vultures, Black storks, and Bonelli's eagles, especially near the dam. Deer and wild boar are also present in the park, though they are more elusive.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes in the area?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with and utilizes sections of several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Sendero de Sierra Morena, PR-A 348, Sendero Los Ángeles, and PR-A 396, offering a varied experience within the natural park.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Santuario de los Angeles y Embalse de Bembazar jogging trail?
faqs.answer: The full round trip for this trail, which is approximately 11.5 km, is estimated to take about 3.5 hours at a walking pace. For jogging, you can expect to complete it in less time, with the Komoot estimate for jogging being around 1 hour and 22 minutes for the full distance.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is a natural path, the nearby Hornachuelos Old Town is likely to offer cafes, restaurants, and public restrooms. It's recommended to visit these facilities before starting your jog, as amenities directly on the trail are limited to natural features like the Fuente de los Tres Caños.

waypoints.name: View of the Seminario de los Ángeles
